headline: MAHB Passenger Service Charge Hike Draws Tourism Backlash
context: MAHB's RM25 increase in international passenger service charge to RM98 per departure, effective April 2026, drew sharp criticism from airlines and tourism bodies who warned it would reduce Malaysia's competitiveness against Thailand and Indonesia.

cards.big: RM98 departure tax makes KL one of Asia's most expensive airports to leave — Bangkok charges RM48 and Bali charges RM62
cards.sub: Average tourist family of 4 pays RM392 just in departure charges — equivalent to 2 nights' budget accommodation

cards.big: PSC increase generates RM1.4 billion additional annual revenue for MAHB — but tourism industry estimates RM3.2 billion in lost visitor spending
cards.sub: Price-sensitive budget travellers — 62% of KLIA2 traffic — most likely to divert to cheaper regional alternatives

cards.big: Tourism employs 3.6 million Malaysians — hotel and retail workers in Langkawi and Penang bear the impact of declining arrivals
cards.sub: AirAsia warned of 8% traffic reduction from PSC hike — translating to 2.4 million fewer passengers annually at KLIA2

cards.big: MAVCOM approved PSC increase without public consultation or published cost-benefit analysis
cards.sub: Airline operator objections submitted during 30-day comment period were overridden without published justification

cards.big: Raising departure taxes to fund airport profits while the tourism industry begs for competitive pricing puts infrastructure revenue above national interest
Should PSC rates be benchmarked against ASEAN competitors and capped at regional median to protect tourism competitiveness?
